1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
3	#
4	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	#
8	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	#
10	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	# limitations under the License.
15	#
16	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
17	#
18	# Bash Console Utility Library
19	
20	set -euo pipefail
21	
22	# Multiple inclusion guard
23	test -z "${ZBUC_INCLUDED:-}" || return 0
24	ZBUC_INCLUDED=1
25	
26	# Color tinder constants (pure ANSI-C quoted literals — no computation, no $())
27	BUC_black=$'\033[1;30m'
28	BUC_red=$'\033[1;31m'
29	BUC_green=$'\033[1;32m'
30	BUC_yellow=$'\033[1;33m'
31	BUC_blue=$'\033[1;34m'
32	BUC_magenta=$'\033[1;35m'
33	BUC_cyan=$'\033[1;36m'
34	BUC_white=$'\033[1;37m'
35	BUC_gray=$'\033[90m'
36	BUC_reset=$'\033[0m'
37	
38	# Global context variable for info and error messages
39	ZBUC_CONTEXT=""
40	
41	# Help mode flag
42	ZBUC_DOC_MODE=false
43	
44	
45	######################################################################
46	# Internal logging helpers
47	
48	# Usage: zbuc_make_tag <depth> "<label>"
49	#   Computes ZBUC_TAG for the given stack depth/label (no I/O).
50	# Usage: zbuc_tag_args <depth> "<label>" [arg...]
51	#   Computes ZBUC_TAG and logs args directly to the transcript.
52	#
53	# Bash stack quirk:
54	#   BASH_SOURCE[i] / FUNCNAME[i] index the current frame,
55	#   but BASH_LINENO[i] reports the line where FUNCNAME[i+1] was called.
56	#   For depth=N callers:
57	#       file = BASH_SOURCE[N]
58	#       line = BASH_LINENO[N-1]
59	# Usage: zbuc_make_tag <depth> "<label>"
60	#   Computes ZBUC_TAG for the given stack depth/label (no I/O).
61	#   Note: With depth=0 or too-deep stacks, file/line may be empty (by design).
62	zbuc_make_tag() {
63	  local z_d="${1:-1}"
64	  case "${z_d}" in ''|*[!0-9]*) z_d=1 ;; esac
65	  local z_label="${2:-}"
66	  local z_file="${BASH_SOURCE[z_d]##*/}"
67	  local z_line="${BASH_LINENO[z_d-1]}"
68	  ZBUC_TAG="${z_label}${z_file}:${z_line}: "
69	}
70	
71	zbuc_tag_args() {
72	  local z_d="${1:-1}"
73	  shift
74	  local z_label="${1:-}"
75	  shift
76	  zbuc_make_tag "${z_d}" "${z_label}"
77	  printf '%s\n' "$@" | zbuc_log "${ZBUC_TAG}" " ---- "
78	}
79	
80	######################################################################
81	# Public logging wrappers
82	
83	buc_log_args() { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_log_args " "$@"; }
84	buc_log_pipe() { zbuc_make_tag 3 "buc_log_pipe "; zbuc_log "${ZBUC_TAG}" " ---- "; }
85	
86	buc_step()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_step     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 "${BUC_white}$*${BUC_reset}"; }
87	buc_code()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_code     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 "${BUC_cyan}$*${BUC_reset}"; }
88	buc_info()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_info     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 "$@"; }
89	buc_debug()    { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_debug    " "$@"; zbuc_print 2 "$@"; }
90	buc_trace()    { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_trace    " "$@"; zbuc_print 3 "$@"; }
91	buc_warn()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_warn     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 "${BUC_yellow}WARNING:${BUC_reset} $*"; }
92	buc_success()  { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_success  " "$@"; printf '%b\n' "${BUC_green}$*${BUC_reset}" >&2 || buc_die; }
93	buc_die() {
94	  zbuc_tag_args                3 "buc_die      " "ERROR: [${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}] $*"
95	  zbuc_print -1          "${BUC_red}ERROR:${BUC_reset} [${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}] $*"
96	  exit 1
97	}
98	
99	# Display unprefixed cyan text for typeable guidance (commands, config values)
100	buc_bare() { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_bare     " "$@"; printf '%b\n' "${BUC_cyan}$*${BUC_reset}" >&2; }
101	
102	# Display tabtarget hint: resolves colophon to tabtarget filename
103	# Args: colophon [extra_args...]
104	buc_tabtarget() {
105	  local z_colophon="$1"
106	  shift
107	  local z_extra="${*:+ $*}"
108	  local z_matches=("${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}"/${z_colophon}.*)
109	  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || buc_die "buc_tabtarget: no tabtarget found for colophon '${z_colophon}'"
110	  buc_bare "        ${z_matches[0]}${z_extra}"
111	}
112	
113	buc_context() {
114	  ZBUC_CONTEXT="$1"
115	}
116	
117	# Enable trace to stderr safely if supported
118	zbuc_enable_trace() {
119	  # Only supported in Bash >= 4.1
120	  if test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -gt 4 || { test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -eq 4 && test "${BASH_VERSINFO[1]}" -ge 1; }; then
121	    export BASH_XTRACEFD=2
122	  fi
123	  set -x
124	}
125	
126	# Disable trace
127	zbuc_disable_trace() {
128	  set +x
129	}
130	
131	zbuc_doc_mode_predicate() {
132	  test "${ZBUC_DOC_MODE}" = "true"
133	}
134	
135	buc_doc_env() {
136	  set -e
137	
138	  local env_var_name="${1}"
139	  local env_var_info="${2}"
140	
141	  # Trim trailing spaces from variable name
142	  env_var_name="${env_var_name%% *}"
143	
144	  # In doc mode, show documentation only (no validation — env vars may not be set)
145	  if zbuc_doc_mode_predicate; then
146	    echo "  ${BUC_magenta}${1}${BUC_reset}:  ${env_var_info}"
147	    return 0
148	  fi
149	
150	  # In execute mode, validate variable is set
151	  test -n "${!env_var_name:-}" || buc_warn "${env_var_name} is not set"
152	}
153	
154	# Idiomatic last step of environment documentation in furnish.
155	# In doc mode, signals furnish to return early (sourcing/kindle not needed for help).
156	# Usage:
157	#    buc_doc_env_done || return 0
158	buc_doc_env_done() {
159	  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
160	  return 1
161	}
162	
163	ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="UNFILLED"
164	
165	buc_doc_brief() {
166	  set -e
167	  ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="${ZBUC_CONTEXT}"
168	  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
169	  echo
170	  echo "  ${BUC_white}${ZBUC_CONTEXT}${BUC_reset}"
171	  echo "    brief: $1"
172	}
173	
174	buc_doc_lines() {
175	  set -e
176	  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
177	  echo "           $1"
178	}
179	
180	buc_doc_param() {
181	  set -e
182	  ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="${ZBUC_USAGE_STRING} <<$1>>"
183	  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
184	  echo "    required: $1 - $2"
185	}
186	
187	buc_doc_oparm() {
188	  set -e
189	  ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="${ZBUC_USAGE_STRING} [<<$1>>]"
190	  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
191	  echo "    optional: $1 - $2"
192	}
193	
194	zbuc_usage() {
195	  printf '%b\n' "    usage: ${BUC_cyan}${ZBUC_USAGE_STRING}${BUC_reset}"
196	}
197	
198	# Idiomatic last step of documentation in the bash api.
199	# Usage:
200	#    buc_doc_shown || return 0
201	buc_doc_shown() {
202	  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
203	  zbuc_usage
204	  return 1
205	}
206	
207	buc_set_doc_mode() {
208	  ZBUC_DOC_MODE=true
209	}
210	
211	buc_usage_die() {
212	  set -e
213	  local context="${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}"
214	  local usage=$(zbuc_usage)
215	  printf '%b\n' "${BUC_red}ERROR:${BUC_reset} ${usage}"
216	  exit 1
217	}
218	
219	# Multi-line print function with verbosity control
220	# Sends output to stderr to avoid interfering with stdout returns
221	zbuc_print() {
222	  local min_verbosity="$1"
223	  shift
224	
225	  # Always print if min_verbosity is -1, otherwise check BURE_VERBOSE
226	  if test "${min_verbosity}" -eq -1 || test "${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}" -ge "${min_verbosity}"; then
227	    while test $# -gt 0; do
228	      if test -n "${ZBUC_CONTEXT}"; then
229	        printf '%b%s%b %s\n' "${BUC_gray}" "${ZBUC_CONTEXT}" "${BUC_reset}" "$1" >&2
230	      else
231	        echo "$1" >&2
232	      fi
233	      shift
234	    done
235	  fi
236	}
237	
238	# Core logging implementation - always reads from stdin
239	zbuc_log() {
240	  test -n "${BURD_TRANSCRIPT:-}" || return 0
241	
242	  local z_prefix="$1"
243	  local z_rest_prefix="$2"
244	  local z_outfile="${BURD_TRANSCRIPT}"
245	
246	  while IFS= read -r z_line; do
247	    printf '%s%s\n' "${z_prefix}" "${z_line}" >> "${z_outfile}"
248	    z_prefix="${z_rest_prefix}"
249	  done
250	}
251	
252	
253	# Die if condition is true (non-zero)
254	# Usage: buc_die_if <condition> <message1> [<message2> ...]
255	buc_die_if() {
256	  local condition="$1"
257	  shift
258	
259	  test "${condition}" -ne 0 || return 0
260	
261	  set -e
262	  local context="${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}"
263	  zbuc_print -1 "${BUC_red}ERROR:${BUC_reset} [$context] $1"
264	  shift
265	  zbuc_print -1 "$@"
266	  exit 1
267	}
268	
269	# Die unless condition is true (zero)
270	# Usage: buc_die_unless <condition> <message1> [<message2> ...]
271	buc_die_unless() {
272	  local condition="$1"
273	  shift
274	
275	  test "${condition}" -eq 0 || return 0
276	
277	  set -e
278	  local context="${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}"
279	  zbuc_print -1 "${BUC_red}ERROR:${BUC_reset} [$context] $1"
280	  shift
281	  zbuc_print -1 "$@"
282	  exit 1
283	}
284	
285	zbuc_show_help() {
286	  local prefix="$1"
287	  local title="$2"
288	  local env_func="$3"
289	
290	  echo "$title"
291	  echo
292	
293	  if test -n "${env_func}"; then
294	    echo "Environment Variables:"
295	    "$env_func"
296	    echo
297	  fi
298	
299	  echo "Commands:"
300	
301	  local z_decl z_flag z_cmd
302	  while read -r z_decl z_flag z_cmd; do
303	    [[ "${z_cmd}" =~ ^${prefix}[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ ]] || continue
304	    buc_context "${z_cmd}"
305	    "${z_cmd}"
306	  done < <(declare -F)
307	}
308	
309	buc_countdown() {
310	  local z_seconds="$1"
311	  local z_prompt="$2"
312	
313	  if test "${BURE_COUNTDOWN:-}" = "skip"; then
314	    buc_step "Countdown: ${z_seconds}s (skipped — BURE_COUNTDOWN=skip)"
315	    return 0
316	  fi
317	
318	  test -z "${BURE_COUNTDOWN:-}" || buc_die "BURE_COUNTDOWN must be 'skip' or unset, got '${BURE_COUNTDOWN}'"
319	
320	  buc_step "Countdown: ${z_seconds}s to cancel (Ctrl-C)"
321	  sleep 1
322	  printf '%b ' "${BUC_yellow}${z_prompt}${BUC_reset}" >/dev/tty
323	  local z_i
324	  for (( z_i=z_seconds; z_i>=1; z_i-- )); do
325	    printf '%d... ' "$z_i" >/dev/tty
326	    sleep 1
327	  done
328	  printf '\n' >/dev/tty
329	}
330	
331	buc_require() {
332	  local z_prompt="$1"
333	  local z_required_value="$2"
334	
335	  if test "${BURE_CONFIRM:-}" = "skip"; then
336	    buc_step "Confirm: (skipped — BURE_CONFIRM=skip)"
337	    return 0
338	  fi
339	
340	  test -z "${BURE_CONFIRM:-}" || buc_die "BURE_CONFIRM must be 'skip' or unset, got '${BURE_CONFIRM}'"
341	
342	  sleep 1
343	  printf '%b\n' "${BUC_yellow}${z_prompt}${BUC_reset}" >/dev/tty
344	  printf 'Type %s to confirm: ' "${z_required_value}" >/dev/tty
345	  local z_input
346	  read -r z_input </dev/tty
347	  test "${z_input}" = "${z_required_value}" || buc_die "Confirmation failed — expected '${z_required_value}', got '${z_input}'"
348	}
349	
350	buc_execute() {
351	  set -e
352	  local prefix="$1"
353	  local title="$2"
354	  local env_func="$3"
355	  local command="${4:-}"
356	  shift 3; test -z "${command}" || shift
357	
358	  export BUC_VERBOSE="${BUC_VERBOSE:-0}"
359	
360	  # Enable bash trace to stderr if BUC_VERBOSE is 3 or higher and bash >= 4.1
361	  if test "${BUC_VERBOSE}" -ge 3; then
362	    if test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -gt 4 || { test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -eq 4 && test "${BASH_VERSINFO[1]}" -ge 1; }; then
363	      export PS4='+ ${BASH_SOURCE##*/}:${LINENO}: '
364	      export BASH_XTRACEFD=2
365	      set -x
366	    fi
367	  fi
368	
369	  # Validate prefix pattern, furnish deps, then dispatch command
370	  if test -n "${command}" && [[ "${command}" =~ ^${prefix}[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ ]]; then
371	    buc_context "${command}"
372	    test -z "${env_func}" || "${env_func}" "${command}"
373	    declare -F "${command}" >/dev/null || buc_die "Function not found: ${command}"
374	    "${command}" "$@"
375	  else
376	    test -z "${command}" || buc_warn "Unknown command: ${command}"
377	    buc_set_doc_mode
378	    zbuc_show_help "${prefix}" "${title}" "${env_func}"
379	    echo
380	    exit 1
381	  fi
382	}
383	
384	# --- Hyperlink helpers (OSC-8), falls back to plain text when disabled ---
385	# Disable with: export BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS=1
386	zbuc_hyperlink() {
387	  local z_text="${1:-}"
388	  local z_url="${2:-}"
389	
390	  # ANSI codes for blue underlined text
391	  local z_blue_underline=$'\033[34m\033[4m'
392	  local z_reset=$'\033[0m'
393	
394	  if test -n "${BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS:-}"; then
395	    # Fallback: blue underlined text with URL in angle brackets
396	    printf '%s%s%s <%s>' "${z_blue_underline}" "${z_text}" "${z_reset}" "${z_url}"
397	    return 0
398	  fi
399	
400	  # OSC-8 with blue underline formatting
401	  printf '%s\033]8;;%s\033\\%s\033]8;;\033\\%s' \
402	    "${z_blue_underline}" "${z_url}" "${z_text}" "${z_reset}"
403	}
404	
405	# buc_link "Prefix text" "Link text" "URL"  (prints to stderr like other user-visible messages)
406	buc_link() {
407	  local z_prefix="${1:-}"
408	  local z_text="${2:-}"
409	  local z_url="${3:-}"
410	
411	  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_link    " "${z_prefix} ${z_text} -> ${z_url}"
412	
413	  # Always show at verbosity >= 0 (same visibility as buc_step)
414	  if test "${BUC_VERBOSE:-0}" -ge 0; then
415	    # Print prefix text if provided
416	    test -n "${z_prefix}" && printf '%s ' "${z_prefix}" >&2
417	
418	    # Print formatted hyperlink
419	    zbuc_hyperlink "${z_text}" "${z_url}" >&2
420	    echo >&2
421	  fi
422	}
423	
424	# eof
425

#!/bin/bash
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Console Utility Library

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUC_INCLUDED:-}" || return 0
ZBUC_INCLUDED=1

# Color tinder constants (pure ANSI-C quoted literals — no computation, no $())
BUC_black=$'\033[1;30m'
BUC_red=$'\033[1;31m'
BUC_green=$'\033[1;32m'
BUC_yellow=$'\033[1;33m'
BUC_blue=$'\033[1;34m'
BUC_magenta=$'\033[1;35m'
BUC_cyan=$'\033[1;36m'
BUC_white=$'\033[1;37m'
BUC_gray=$'\033[90m'
BUC_reset=$'\033[0m'

# Global context variable for info and error messages
ZBUC_CONTEXT=""

# Help mode flag
ZBUC_DOC_MODE=false


######################################################################
# Internal logging helpers

# Usage: zbuc_make_tag <depth> "<label>"
#   Computes ZBUC_TAG for the given stack depth/label (no I/O).
# Usage: zbuc_tag_args <depth> "<label>" [arg...]
#   Computes ZBUC_TAG and logs args directly to the transcript.
#
# Bash stack quirk:
#   BASH_SOURCE[i] / FUNCNAME[i] index the current frame,
#   but BASH_LINENO[i] reports the line where FUNCNAME[i+1] was called.
#   For depth=N callers:
#       file = BASH_SOURCE[N]
#       line = BASH_LINENO[N-1]
# Usage: zbuc_make_tag <depth> "<label>"
#   Computes ZBUC_TAG for the given stack depth/label (no I/O).
#   Note: With depth=0 or too-deep stacks, file/line may be empty (by design).
zbuc_make_tag() {
  local z_d="${1:-1}"
  case "${z_d}" in ''|*[!0-9]*) z_d=1 ;; esac
  local z_label="${2:-}"
  local z_file="${BASH_SOURCE[z_d]##*/}"
  local z_line="${BASH_LINENO[z_d-1]}"
  ZBUC_TAG="${z_label}${z_file}:${z_line}: "
}

zbuc_tag_args() {
  local z_d="${1:-1}"
  shift
  local z_label="${1:-}"
  shift
  zbuc_make_tag "${z_d}" "${z_label}"
  printf '%s\n' "$@" | zbuc_log "${ZBUC_TAG}" " ---- "
}

######################################################################
# Public logging wrappers

buc_log_args() { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_log_args " "$@"; }
buc_log_pipe() { zbuc_make_tag 3 "buc_log_pipe "; zbuc_log "${ZBUC_TAG}" " ---- "; }

buc_step()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_step     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 "${BUC_white}$*${BUC_reset}"; }
buc_code()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_code     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 "${BUC_cyan}$*${BUC_reset}"; }
buc_info()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_info     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 "$@"; }
buc_debug()    { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_debug    " "$@"; zbuc_print 2 "$@"; }
buc_trace()    { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_trace    " "$@"; zbuc_print 3 "$@"; }
buc_warn()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_warn     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 "${BUC_yellow}WARNING:${BUC_reset} $*"; }
buc_success()  { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_success  " "$@"; printf '%b\n' "${BUC_green}$*${BUC_reset}" >&2 || buc_die; }
buc_die() {
  zbuc_tag_args                3 "buc_die      " "ERROR: [${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}] $*"
  zbuc_print -1          "${BUC_red}ERROR:${BUC_reset} [${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}] $*"
  exit 1
}

# Display unprefixed cyan text for typeable guidance (commands, config values)
buc_bare() { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_bare     " "$@"; printf '%b\n' "${BUC_cyan}$*${BUC_reset}" >&2; }

# Display tabtarget hint: resolves colophon to tabtarget filename
# Args: colophon [extra_args...]
buc_tabtarget() {
  local z_colophon="$1"
  shift
  local z_extra="${*:+ $*}"
  local z_matches=("${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}"/${z_colophon}.*)
  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || buc_die "buc_tabtarget: no tabtarget found for colophon '${z_colophon}'"
  buc_bare "        ${z_matches[0]}${z_extra}"
}

buc_context() {
  ZBUC_CONTEXT="$1"
}

# Enable trace to stderr safely if supported
zbuc_enable_trace() {
  # Only supported in Bash >= 4.1
  if test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -gt 4 || { test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -eq 4 && test "${BASH_VERSINFO[1]}" -ge 1; }; then
    export BASH_XTRACEFD=2
  fi
  set -x
}

# Disable trace
zbuc_disable_trace() {
  set +x
}

zbuc_doc_mode_predicate() {
  test "${ZBUC_DOC_MODE}" = "true"
}

buc_doc_env() {
  set -e

  local env_var_name="${1}"
  local env_var_info="${2}"

  # Trim trailing spaces from variable name
  env_var_name="${env_var_name%% *}"

  # In doc mode, show documentation only (no validation — env vars may not be set)
  if zbuc_doc_mode_predicate; then
    echo "  ${BUC_magenta}${1}${BUC_reset}:  ${env_var_info}"
    return 0
  fi

  # In execute mode, validate variable is set
  test -n "${!env_var_name:-}" || buc_warn "${env_var_name} is not set"
}

# Idiomatic last step of environment documentation in furnish.
# In doc mode, signals furnish to return early (sourcing/kindle not needed for help).
# Usage:
#    buc_doc_env_done || return 0
buc_doc_env_done() {
  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
  return 1
}

ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="UNFILLED"

buc_doc_brief() {
  set -e
  ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="${ZBUC_CONTEXT}"
  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
  echo
  echo "  ${BUC_white}${ZBUC_CONTEXT}${BUC_reset}"
  echo "    brief: $1"
}

buc_doc_lines() {
  set -e
  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
  echo "           $1"
}

buc_doc_param() {
  set -e
  ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="${ZBUC_USAGE_STRING} <<$1>>"
  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
  echo "    required: $1 - $2"
}

buc_doc_oparm() {
  set -e
  ZBUC_USAGE_STRING="${ZBUC_USAGE_STRING} [<<$1>>]"
  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
  echo "    optional: $1 - $2"
}

zbuc_usage() {
  printf '%b\n' "    usage: ${BUC_cyan}${ZBUC_USAGE_STRING}${BUC_reset}"
}

# Idiomatic last step of documentation in the bash api.
# Usage:
#    buc_doc_shown || return 0
buc_doc_shown() {
  zbuc_doc_mode_predicate || return 0
  zbuc_usage
  return 1
}

buc_set_doc_mode() {
  ZBUC_DOC_MODE=true
}

buc_usage_die() {
  set -e
  local context="${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}"
  local usage=$(zbuc_usage)
  printf '%b\n' "${BUC_red}ERROR:${BUC_reset} ${usage}"
  exit 1
}

# Multi-line print function with verbosity control
# Sends output to stderr to avoid interfering with stdout returns
zbuc_print() {
  local min_verbosity="$1"
  shift

  # Always print if min_verbosity is -1, otherwise check BURE_VERBOSE
  if test "${min_verbosity}" -eq -1 || test "${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}" -ge "${min_verbosity}"; then
    while test $# -gt 0; do
      if test -n "${ZBUC_CONTEXT}"; then
        printf '%b%s%b %s\n' "${BUC_gray}" "${ZBUC_CONTEXT}" "${BUC_reset}" "$1" >&2
      else
        echo "$1" >&2
      fi
      shift
    done
  fi
}

# Core logging implementation - always reads from stdin
zbuc_log() {
  test -n "${BURD_TRANSCRIPT:-}" || return 0

  local z_prefix="$1"
  local z_rest_prefix="$2"
  local z_outfile="${BURD_TRANSCRIPT}"

  while IFS= read -r z_line; do
    printf '%s%s\n' "${z_prefix}" "${z_line}" >> "${z_outfile}"
    z_prefix="${z_rest_prefix}"
  done
}


# Die if condition is true (non-zero)
# Usage: buc_die_if <condition> <message1> [<message2> ...]
buc_die_if() {
  local condition="$1"
  shift

  test "${condition}" -ne 0 || return 0

  set -e
  local context="${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}"
  zbuc_print -1 "${BUC_red}ERROR:${BUC_reset} [$context] $1"
  shift
  zbuc_print -1 "$@"
  exit 1
}

# Die unless condition is true (zero)
# Usage: buc_die_unless <condition> <message1> [<message2> ...]
buc_die_unless() {
  local condition="$1"
  shift

  test "${condition}" -eq 0 || return 0

  set -e
  local context="${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}"
  zbuc_print -1 "${BUC_red}ERROR:${BUC_reset} [$context] $1"
  shift
  zbuc_print -1 "$@"
  exit 1
}

zbuc_show_help() {
  local prefix="$1"
  local title="$2"
  local env_func="$3"

  echo "$title"
  echo

  if test -n "${env_func}"; then
    echo "Environment Variables:"
    "$env_func"
    echo
  fi

  echo "Commands:"

  local z_decl z_flag z_cmd
  while read -r z_decl z_flag z_cmd; do
    [[ "${z_cmd}" =~ ^${prefix}[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ ]] || continue
    buc_context "${z_cmd}"
    "${z_cmd}"
  done < <(declare -F)
}

buc_countdown() {
  local z_seconds="$1"
  local z_prompt="$2"

  if test "${BURE_COUNTDOWN:-}" = "skip"; then
    buc_step "Countdown: ${z_seconds}s (skipped — BURE_COUNTDOWN=skip)"
    return 0
  fi

  test -z "${BURE_COUNTDOWN:-}" || buc_die "BURE_COUNTDOWN must be 'skip' or unset, got '${BURE_COUNTDOWN}'"

  buc_step "Countdown: ${z_seconds}s to cancel (Ctrl-C)"
  sleep 1
  printf '%b ' "${BUC_yellow}${z_prompt}${BUC_reset}" >/dev/tty
  local z_i
  for (( z_i=z_seconds; z_i>=1; z_i-- )); do
    printf '%d... ' "$z_i" >/dev/tty
    sleep 1
  done
  printf '\n' >/dev/tty
}

buc_require() {
  local z_prompt="$1"
  local z_required_value="$2"

  if test "${BURE_CONFIRM:-}" = "skip"; then
    buc_step "Confirm: (skipped — BURE_CONFIRM=skip)"
    return 0
  fi

  test -z "${BURE_CONFIRM:-}" || buc_die "BURE_CONFIRM must be 'skip' or unset, got '${BURE_CONFIRM}'"

  sleep 1
  printf '%b\n' "${BUC_yellow}${z_prompt}${BUC_reset}" >/dev/tty
  printf 'Type %s to confirm: ' "${z_required_value}" >/dev/tty
  local z_input
  read -r z_input </dev/tty
  test "${z_input}" = "${z_required_value}" || buc_die "Confirmation failed — expected '${z_required_value}', got '${z_input}'"
}

buc_execute() {
  set -e
  local prefix="$1"
  local title="$2"
  local env_func="$3"
  local command="${4:-}"
  shift 3; test -z "${command}" || shift

  export BUC_VERBOSE="${BUC_VERBOSE:-0}"

  # Enable bash trace to stderr if BUC_VERBOSE is 3 or higher and bash >= 4.1
  if test "${BUC_VERBOSE}" -ge 3; then
    if test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -gt 4 || { test "${BASH_VERSINFO[0]}" -eq 4 && test "${BASH_VERSINFO[1]}" -ge 1; }; then
      export PS4='+ ${BASH_SOURCE##*/}:${LINENO}: '
      export BASH_XTRACEFD=2
      set -x
    fi
  fi

  # Validate prefix pattern, furnish deps, then dispatch command
  if test -n "${command}" && [[ "${command}" =~ ^${prefix}[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$ ]]; then
    buc_context "${command}"
    test -z "${env_func}" || "${env_func}" "${command}"
    declare -F "${command}" >/dev/null || buc_die "Function not found: ${command}"
    "${command}" "$@"
  else
    test -z "${command}" || buc_warn "Unknown command: ${command}"
    buc_set_doc_mode
    zbuc_show_help "${prefix}" "${title}" "${env_func}"
    echo
    exit 1
  fi
}

# --- Hyperlink helpers (OSC-8), falls back to plain text when disabled ---
# Disable with: export BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS=1
zbuc_hyperlink() {
  local z_text="${1:-}"
  local z_url="${2:-}"

  # ANSI codes for blue underlined text
  local z_blue_underline=$'\033[34m\033[4m'
  local z_reset=$'\033[0m'

  if test -n "${BURD_NO_HYPERLINKS:-}"; then
    # Fallback: blue underlined text with URL in angle brackets
    printf '%s%s%s <%s>' "${z_blue_underline}" "${z_text}" "${z_reset}" "${z_url}"
    return 0
  fi

  # OSC-8 with blue underline formatting
  printf '%s\033]8;;%s\033\\%s\033]8;;\033\\%s' \
    "${z_blue_underline}" "${z_url}" "${z_text}" "${z_reset}"
}

# buc_link "Prefix text" "Link text" "URL"  (prints to stderr like other user-visible messages)
buc_link() {
  local z_prefix="${1:-}"
  local z_text="${2:-}"
  local z_url="${3:-}"

  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_link    " "${z_prefix} ${z_text} -> ${z_url}"

  # Always show at verbosity >= 0 (same visibility as buc_step)
  if test "${BUC_VERBOSE:-0}" -ge 0; then
    # Print prefix text if provided
    test -n "${z_prefix}" && printf '%s ' "${z_prefix}" >&2

    # Print formatted hyperlink
    zbuc_hyperlink "${z_text}" "${z_url}" >&2
    echo >&2
  fi
}

25	set -euo pipefail
26	
27	# Multiple inclusion detection
28	test -z "${ZBUHW_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhw multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
29	ZBUHW_SOURCED=1

31	######################################################################
32	# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel

34	zbuhw_kindle() {
35	  test -z "${ZBUHW_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhw already kindled"
36	
37	  # Tinder — fixed Windows paths and network constants
38	  # Double-backslash for correct rendering through printf %b
39	  readonly ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG='C:\\ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config'
40	  readonly ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS='C:\\ProgramData\\ssh\\administrators_authorized_keys'
41	  readonly ZBUHW_CYGWIN_ROOT='C:\\cygwin64'
42	  readonly ZBUHW_CYGWIN_BASH='C:\\cygwin64\\bin\\bash.exe'
43	  readonly ZBUHW_SSH_PORT="22"
44	  readonly ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME="sshd"
45	  readonly ZBUHW_FW_DISPLAY_NAME="OpenSSH Server"
46	
47	  readonly ZBUHW_KINDLED=1
48	}
49	
50	zbuhw_sentinel() {
51	  test "${ZBUHW_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buhw not kindled - call zbuhw_kindle first"
52	}
53	
54	######################################################################
55	# External Functions (buhw_*)
56	
57	buhw_access_base() {
58	  zbuhw_sentinel
59	
60	  buc_doc_brief "Display OpenSSH server installation and lockdown procedure"
61	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
62	
63	  buh_section  "OpenSSH Server Installation & Lockdown"
64	  buh_t        "Establish Windows as a keys-only SSH endpoint."
65	  buh_e
66	  buh_section  "Preconditions:"
67	  buh_t        "- Windows host with administrator access"
68	  buh_t        "- Network reachable on TCP/${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}"
69	  buh_e
70	  buh_step1    "Open Elevated PowerShell:"
71	  buh_t        "Right-click Start → Terminal (Admin), or search 'PowerShell' and Run as Administrator."
72	  buh_t        "All commands below run in this elevated session."
73	  buh_e
74	  buh_step1    "Install OpenSSH Server:"
75	  buh_t        "This downloads from Windows Update — may take 10+ minutes."
76	  buh_c        "Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0"
77	  buh_e
78	  buh_step1    "Enable OpenSSH Server:"
79	  buh_c        "Start-Service sshd"
80	  buh_c        "Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic"
81	  buh_e
82	  buh_step1    "Allow Port ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT} Through Firewall:"
83	  buh_c        "New-NetFirewallRule -Name ${ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME} -DisplayName \"${ZBUHW_FW_DISPLAY_NAME}\" -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}"
84	  buh_e
85	  buh_step1    "Configure sshd_config:"
86	  buh_tc       "File: " "${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG}"
87	  buh_t        "This file is SYSTEM-owned. Edit a temp copy, then replace."
88	  buh_e
89	  buh_step2    "Copy to editable location:"
90	  buh_c        "Copy-Item ${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG} \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
91	  buh_e
92	  buh_step2    "Edit the copy:"
93	  buh_c        "notepad \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
94	  buh_t        "Set these directives (add if not present):"
95	  buh_c        "PasswordAuthentication no"
96	  buh_c        "PubkeyAuthentication yes"
97	  buh_c        "PermitEmptyPasswords no"
98	  buh_t        "Do NOT add UsePAM or ChallengeResponseAuthentication — Windows OpenSSH"
99	  buh_t        "rejects unrecognized directives and the service will fail to start."
100	  buh_e
101	  buh_step2    "Validate before applying:"
102	  buh_c        "sshd -t -f \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
103	  buh_t        "Expect: no output (silence means valid). Fix any reported errors."
104	  buh_e
105	  buh_step2    "Replace the original:"
106	  buh_c        "Copy-Item \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config ${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG} -Force"
107	  buh_e
108	  buh_step1    "Restart Service:"
109	  buh_c        "Restart-Service sshd"
110	  buh_e
111	  buh_step1    "Verify:"
112	  buh_t        "Discover your Windows username and IP (on the Windows machine):"
113	  buh_c        "whoami"
114	  buh_c        "ipconfig"
115	  buh_t        "Note the username (after the backslash) and IPv4 address."
116	  buh_e
117	  buh_t        "From a remote machine, test SSH reachability:"
118	  buh_c        "ssh <username>@<ip>"
119	  buh_t        "Expect: Permission denied (publickey). This confirms sshd is running"
120	  buh_t        "and password login is correctly rejected. Key setup follows in step 2."
121	
122	}
123	
124	buhw_access_remote() {
125	  zbuhw_sentinel
126	
127	  buc_doc_brief "Display SSH client key generation and host config procedure (args: host user key-name alias)"
128	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
129	
130	  local z_host="${1:-}"
131	  local z_user="${2:-}"
132	  local z_key_name="${3:-}"
133	  local z_alias="${4:-}"
134	
135	  test -n "${z_host}"     || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: host required (arg 1)"
136	  test -n "${z_user}"     || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: user required (arg 2)"
137	  test -n "${z_key_name}" || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: key-name required (arg 3)"
138	  test -n "${z_alias}"    || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: alias required (arg 4)"
139	
140	  buh_section  "SSH Client Key & Host Configuration"
141	  buh_t        "Provision client with a dedicated key and deterministic host config."
142	  buh_e
143	  buh_section  "Parameters:"
144	  buh_tc       "  Host:     " "${z_host}"
145	  buh_tc       "  User:     " "${z_user}"
146	  buh_tc       "  Key name: " "${z_key_name}"
147	  buh_tc       "  Alias:    " "${z_alias}"
148	  buh_e
149	  buh_step1    "Generate Key:"
150	  buh_c        "ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/${z_key_name}"
151	  buh_e
152	  buh_step1    "Create SSH Config Entry:"
153	  buh_tc       "Add to " "~/.ssh/config"
154	  buh_e
155	  buh_c        "Host ${z_alias}"
156	  buh_c        "  HostName ${z_host}"
157	  buh_c        "  User ${z_user}"
158	  buh_c        "  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/${z_key_name}"
159	  buh_e
160	  buh_step1    "Copy Public Key to Host:"
161	  buh_t        "The public key must be added to the host's authorized_keys."
162	  buh_tc       "Display the key: " "cat ~/.ssh/${z_key_name}.pub"
163	  buh_t        "Copy the output for use in the entrypoints procedure."
164	  buh_e
165	  buh_section  "Verification:"
166	  buh_c        "ssh ${z_alias}"
167	  buh_t        "Expect: connects (may not yet enter target env — routing not configured)."
168	
169	}
170	
171	buhw_access_entrypoints() {
172	  zbuhw_sentinel
173	
174	  buc_doc_brief "Display SSH command= routing format and key permissions procedure"
175	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
176	
177	  buh_section  "SSH Entrypoint Routing via command= Prefix"
178	  buh_t        "Deterministically route SSH keys to specific environments."
179	  buh_t        "Each key forces a single environment — no interactive shell selection."
180	  buh_e
181	  buh_section  "Preconditions:"
182	  buh_t        "- OpenSSH server installed (access-base complete)"
183	  buh_t        "- Public keys available from access-remote"
184	  buh_e
185	  buh_step1    "Edit Administrators Authorized Keys:"
186	  buh_tc       "File: " "${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}"
187	  buh_t        "Add one line per environment, prefixed with command= directive:"
188	  buh_e
189	  buh_c        "command=\"${ZBUHW_CYGWIN_BASH} -l\" ssh-ed25519 AAAA... cygwin"
190	  buh_c        "command=\"wsl.exe -d DISTRO\"        ssh-ed25519 BBBB... wsl"
191	  buh_c        "command=\"powershell.exe\"            ssh-ed25519 CCCC... windows"
192	  buh_e
193	  buh_tut      "Replace " "DISTRO" " with your WSL distribution name."
194	  buh_tut      "Replace " "AAAA.../BBBB.../CCCC..." " with actual public key content."
195	  buh_e
196	  buh_step1    "Set File Permissions:"
197	  buh_t        "Run in an elevated PowerShell:"
198	  buh_c        "icacls \"${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}\" /inheritance:r"
199	  buh_c        "icacls \"${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}\" /grant \"Administrators:F\""
200	  buh_e
201	  buh_section  "Verification:"
202	  buh_t        "Test each key lands in the correct environment:"
203	  buh_c        "ssh -i key-cygwin host   # lands in Cygwin bash"
204	  buh_c        "ssh -i key-wsl host      # lands in WSL"
205	  buh_c        "ssh -i key-win host      # lands in PowerShell"
206	
207	}
208	
209	buhw_environment_wsl() {
210	  zbuhw_sentinel
211	
212	  buc_doc_brief "Display WSL distribution creation procedure (arg: distro-name)"
213	  buc_doc_shown || return 0

216	  test -n "${z_distro}" || buc_die "buhw_environment_wsl: distro-name required"

220	  buh_e

222	  buh_t        "Run in an elevated PowerShell:"
223	  buh_c        "wsl --install"
224	  buh_e
225	  buh_step1    "Install Base Ubuntu:"
226	  buh_c        "wsl --install -d Ubuntu"
227	  buh_e
228	  buh_step1    "Export and Re-import as Named Distribution:"
229	  buh_c        "wsl --export Ubuntu ubuntu.tar"
230	  buh_c        "wsl --import ${z_distro} C:\\\\WSL\\\\${z_distro} ubuntu.tar"
231	  buh_e
232	  buh_step1    "Enable systemd:"
233	  buh_tct      "Inside the distro (wsl -d " "${z_distro}" "):"
234	  buh_c        "sudo nano /etc/wsl.conf"
235	  buh_t        "Add:"
236	  buh_c        "[boot]"
237	  buh_c        "systemd=true"
238	  buh_e
239	  buh_step1    "Restart WSL:"
240	  buh_c        "wsl --shutdown"
241	  buh_e
242	  buh_section  "Verification:"
243	  buh_c        "wsl -d ${z_distro} systemctl is-system-running"
244	  buh_t        "Expect: running or degraded (acceptable)."
245	
246	}

248	buhw_environment_cygwin() {
249	  zbuhw_sentinel
250	
251	  buc_doc_brief "Display Cygwin POSIX userland installation procedure"
252	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
253

255	  buh_t        "Install POSIX userland for orchestration testing."

259	  buh_tc       "Install to: " "${ZBUHW_CYGWIN_ROOT}"
260	  buh_e

262	  buh_t        "Select these during installation:"
263	  buh_c        "bash"

266	  buh_e

269	  buh_c        "${ZBUHW_CYGWIN_BASH} -l"
270	  buh_e
271	  buh_section  "Verification:"
272	  buh_c        "openssl version"
273	  buh_c        "bash --version"
274	  buh_t        "Expect: bash version >= 3.2"
275	
276	}
277

279	  zbuhw_sentinel
280	
281	  buc_doc_brief "Display BUK top-level handbook index"
282	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
283	
284	  buh_section  "Bash Utility Kit Handbook"
285	  buh_t        "OS-level procedures across all platforms."
286	  buh_e
287	  buh_index_buk
288	
289	}

292	  zbuhw_sentinel
293	
294	  buc_doc_brief "Display BUK-level Windows OS procedures checklist"
295	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
296	
297	  buh_section  "Windows OS Procedures (BUK)"
298	  buh_t        "Generic Windows mechanisms — SSH access, WSL, and Cygwin."
299	  buh_t        "These procedures are OS-level and project-independent."
300	  buh_e
301	  buh_section  "Access Procedures:"
302	  buh_tT       "  1. OpenSSH server install & lockdown:  " "buw-HWab"
303	  buh_tT       "  2. SSH client key & host config:       " "buw-HWar"
304	  buh_tT       "  3. SSH entrypoint routing (command=):  " "buw-HWax"
305	  buh_e
306	  buh_section  "Environment Procedures:"
307	  buh_tT       "  4. WSL distribution setup:             " "buw-HWew"
308	  buh_tT       "  5. Cygwin installation:                " "buw-HWec"

310	}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Utility Handbook - Windows OS Procedures
#
# Generic Windows mechanisms for SSH access, WSL, and Cygwin setup.
# All output is handbook display (buh_* combinators only).
# Project-specific topology and Docker policy live in rbhw_windows.sh.

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBUHW_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhw multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBUHW_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal: Kindle and Sentinel

zbuhw_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUHW_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buhw already kindled"

  # Tinder — fixed Windows paths and network constants
  # Double-backslash for correct rendering through printf %b
  readonly ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG='C:\\ProgramData\\ssh\\sshd_config'
  readonly ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS='C:\\ProgramData\\ssh\\administrators_authorized_keys'
  readonly ZBUHW_CYGWIN_ROOT='C:\\cygwin64'
  readonly ZBUHW_CYGWIN_BASH='C:\\cygwin64\\bin\\bash.exe'
  readonly ZBUHW_SSH_PORT="22"
  readonly ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME="sshd"
  readonly ZBUHW_FW_DISPLAY_NAME="OpenSSH Server"

  readonly ZBUHW_KINDLED=1
}

zbuhw_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUHW_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buhw not kindled - call zbuhw_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (buhw_*)

buhw_access_base() {
  zbuhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display OpenSSH server installation and lockdown procedure"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buh_section  "OpenSSH Server Installation & Lockdown"
  buh_t        "Establish Windows as a keys-only SSH endpoint."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Preconditions:"
  buh_t        "- Windows host with administrator access"
  buh_t        "- Network reachable on TCP/${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Open Elevated PowerShell:"
  buh_t        "Right-click Start → Terminal (Admin), or search 'PowerShell' and Run as Administrator."
  buh_t        "All commands below run in this elevated session."
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Install OpenSSH Server:"
  buh_t        "This downloads from Windows Update — may take 10+ minutes."
  buh_c        "Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Enable OpenSSH Server:"
  buh_c        "Start-Service sshd"
  buh_c        "Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Allow Port ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT} Through Firewall:"
  buh_c        "New-NetFirewallRule -Name ${ZBUHW_FW_RULE_NAME} -DisplayName \"${ZBUHW_FW_DISPLAY_NAME}\" -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort ${ZBUHW_SSH_PORT}"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Configure sshd_config:"
  buh_tc       "File: " "${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG}"
  buh_t        "This file is SYSTEM-owned. Edit a temp copy, then replace."
  buh_e
  buh_step2    "Copy to editable location:"
  buh_c        "Copy-Item ${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG} \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
  buh_e
  buh_step2    "Edit the copy:"
  buh_c        "notepad \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
  buh_t        "Set these directives (add if not present):"
  buh_c        "PasswordAuthentication no"
  buh_c        "PubkeyAuthentication yes"
  buh_c        "PermitEmptyPasswords no"
  buh_t        "Do NOT add UsePAM or ChallengeResponseAuthentication — Windows OpenSSH"
  buh_t        "rejects unrecognized directives and the service will fail to start."
  buh_e
  buh_step2    "Validate before applying:"
  buh_c        "sshd -t -f \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config"
  buh_t        "Expect: no output (silence means valid). Fix any reported errors."
  buh_e
  buh_step2    "Replace the original:"
  buh_c        "Copy-Item \$env:TEMP\\sshd_config ${ZBUHW_SSHD_CONFIG} -Force"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Restart Service:"
  buh_c        "Restart-Service sshd"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Verify:"
  buh_t        "Discover your Windows username and IP (on the Windows machine):"
  buh_c        "whoami"
  buh_c        "ipconfig"
  buh_t        "Note the username (after the backslash) and IPv4 address."
  buh_e
  buh_t        "From a remote machine, test SSH reachability:"
  buh_c        "ssh <username>@<ip>"
  buh_t        "Expect: Permission denied (publickey). This confirms sshd is running"
  buh_t        "and password login is correctly rejected. Key setup follows in step 2."

}

buhw_access_remote() {
  zbuhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display SSH client key generation and host config procedure (args: host user key-name alias)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_host="${1:-}"
  local z_user="${2:-}"
  local z_key_name="${3:-}"
  local z_alias="${4:-}"

  test -n "${z_host}"     || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: host required (arg 1)"
  test -n "${z_user}"     || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: user required (arg 2)"
  test -n "${z_key_name}" || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: key-name required (arg 3)"
  test -n "${z_alias}"    || buc_die "buhw_access_remote: alias required (arg 4)"

  buh_section  "SSH Client Key & Host Configuration"
  buh_t        "Provision client with a dedicated key and deterministic host config."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Parameters:"
  buh_tc       "  Host:     " "${z_host}"
  buh_tc       "  User:     " "${z_user}"
  buh_tc       "  Key name: " "${z_key_name}"
  buh_tc       "  Alias:    " "${z_alias}"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Generate Key:"
  buh_c        "ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/${z_key_name}"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Create SSH Config Entry:"
  buh_tc       "Add to " "~/.ssh/config"
  buh_e
  buh_c        "Host ${z_alias}"
  buh_c        "  HostName ${z_host}"
  buh_c        "  User ${z_user}"
  buh_c        "  IdentityFile ~/.ssh/${z_key_name}"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Copy Public Key to Host:"
  buh_t        "The public key must be added to the host's authorized_keys."
  buh_tc       "Display the key: " "cat ~/.ssh/${z_key_name}.pub"
  buh_t        "Copy the output for use in the entrypoints procedure."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Verification:"
  buh_c        "ssh ${z_alias}"
  buh_t        "Expect: connects (may not yet enter target env — routing not configured)."

}

buhw_access_entrypoints() {
  zbuhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display SSH command= routing format and key permissions procedure"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buh_section  "SSH Entrypoint Routing via command= Prefix"
  buh_t        "Deterministically route SSH keys to specific environments."
  buh_t        "Each key forces a single environment — no interactive shell selection."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Preconditions:"
  buh_t        "- OpenSSH server installed (access-base complete)"
  buh_t        "- Public keys available from access-remote"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Edit Administrators Authorized Keys:"
  buh_tc       "File: " "${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}"
  buh_t        "Add one line per environment, prefixed with command= directive:"
  buh_e
  buh_c        "command=\"${ZBUHW_CYGWIN_BASH} -l\" ssh-ed25519 AAAA... cygwin"
  buh_c        "command=\"wsl.exe -d DISTRO\"        ssh-ed25519 BBBB... wsl"
  buh_c        "command=\"powershell.exe\"            ssh-ed25519 CCCC... windows"
  buh_e
  buh_tut      "Replace " "DISTRO" " with your WSL distribution name."
  buh_tut      "Replace " "AAAA.../BBBB.../CCCC..." " with actual public key content."
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Set File Permissions:"
  buh_t        "Run in an elevated PowerShell:"
  buh_c        "icacls \"${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}\" /inheritance:r"
  buh_c        "icacls \"${ZBUHW_ADMIN_AUTH_KEYS}\" /grant \"Administrators:F\""
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Verification:"
  buh_t        "Test each key lands in the correct environment:"
  buh_c        "ssh -i key-cygwin host   # lands in Cygwin bash"
  buh_c        "ssh -i key-wsl host      # lands in WSL"
  buh_c        "ssh -i key-win host      # lands in PowerShell"

}

buhw_environment_wsl() {
  zbuhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display WSL distribution creation procedure (arg: distro-name)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_distro="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
  test -n "${z_distro}" || buc_die "buhw_environment_wsl: distro-name required"

  buh_section  "WSL Distribution Setup"
  buh_tc       "Create canonical Linux environment: " "${z_distro}"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Enable WSL (if not already):"
  buh_t        "Run in an elevated PowerShell:"
  buh_c        "wsl --install"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Install Base Ubuntu:"
  buh_c        "wsl --install -d Ubuntu"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Export and Re-import as Named Distribution:"
  buh_c        "wsl --export Ubuntu ubuntu.tar"
  buh_c        "wsl --import ${z_distro} C:\\\\WSL\\\\${z_distro} ubuntu.tar"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Enable systemd:"
  buh_tct      "Inside the distro (wsl -d " "${z_distro}" "):"
  buh_c        "sudo nano /etc/wsl.conf"
  buh_t        "Add:"
  buh_c        "[boot]"
  buh_c        "systemd=true"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Restart WSL:"
  buh_c        "wsl --shutdown"
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Verification:"
  buh_c        "wsl -d ${z_distro} systemctl is-system-running"
  buh_t        "Expect: running or degraded (acceptable)."

}

buhw_environment_cygwin() {
  zbuhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display Cygwin POSIX userland installation procedure"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buh_section  "Cygwin Installation"
  buh_t        "Install POSIX userland for orchestration testing."
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Download and Run Cygwin Installer:"
  buh_link     "" "Cygwin Setup (64-bit)" "https://cygwin.com/setup-x86_64.exe"
  buh_tc       "Install to: " "${ZBUHW_CYGWIN_ROOT}"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Required Packages:"
  buh_t        "Select these during installation:"
  buh_c        "bash"
  buh_c        "openssl"
  buh_c        "curl"
  buh_e
  buh_step1    "Verify Installation:"
  buh_t        "Launch Cygwin bash:"
  buh_c        "${ZBUHW_CYGWIN_BASH} -l"
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Verification:"
  buh_c        "openssl version"
  buh_c        "bash --version"
  buh_t        "Expect: bash version >= 3.2"

}

buhw_handbook_top() {
  zbuhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display BUK top-level handbook index"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buh_section  "Bash Utility Kit Handbook"
  buh_t        "OS-level procedures across all platforms."
  buh_e
  buh_index_buk

}

buhw_top() {
  zbuhw_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Display BUK-level Windows OS procedures checklist"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buh_section  "Windows OS Procedures (BUK)"
  buh_t        "Generic Windows mechanisms — SSH access, WSL, and Cygwin."
  buh_t        "These procedures are OS-level and project-independent."
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Access Procedures:"
  buh_tT       "  1. OpenSSH server install & lockdown:  " "buw-HWab"
  buh_tT       "  2. SSH client key & host config:       " "buw-HWar"
  buh_tT       "  3. SSH entrypoint routing (command=):  " "buw-HWax"
  buh_e
  buh_section  "Environment Procedures:"
  buh_tT       "  4. WSL distribution setup:             " "buw-HWew"
  buh_tT       "  5. Cygwin installation:                " "buw-HWec"

}

# eof

4	// tag::mapping-section[]
5	//
6	// Category declarations:
7	// bust_:  Tabtarget (launcher naming vesture)
8	// busd:   Dispatch (non-terminal, tabtarget system umbrella)

10	// busdf_: Dispatch Frontispiece (human-readable description)
11	// busdi_: Dispatch Imprint (embedded parameter/target specifier)
12	// busdm_: Dispatch Formulary (routing component)
13	// busdl_: Dispatch Launcher (bootstrap script)
14	// bukro_: Kit Regime Operation (kindle/validate/render)
15	// burc_:  Configuration Regime (project-level settings)
16	// burs_:  Station Regime (developer-level settings)
17	// burd_:  Dispatch Runtime (ephemeral state assembled during dispatch)
18	// bure_:  Environment Regime (ambient caller-supplied behavioral overrides)
19	// busi:   Invocation (test invocation umbrella)
20	// busit_: Invocation Tier (tier classification)
21	// busig_: Invocation Global (capture state set by zbuto_invoke)
22	// busx_:  Case Boundary (test case isolation runner)
23	// busff_: Fact-File (inter-step communication via output files)
24	// buf_:   Fact Module (dual-write fact-file primitives)
25	// burx_:  Exchange Regime (cross-project dispatch state)

739:{burh_regime}::
740-Per-user, per-connection-profile regime for SSH access to remote hosts.
741:Unlike singleton regimes, BURH is multi-instance: one assignment file per SSH connection,
742:stored at `.buk/users/{burs_user}/<alias>/burh.env`.

765:{burh_ssh_pubkey}::
766-Full SSH public key line (e.g., `ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host`).

772:{burh_command}::

774-Determines which shell or environment the connection launches

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BUK Zipper - Colophon registry via parallel arrays with regime-selection channels
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	test -z "${ZBUZ_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buz multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZBUZ_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Internal kindle boilerplate
29	
30	zbuz_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZBUZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "buz already kindled"
32	
33	  # Registry rolls (populated by buz_enroll in consumer kindle, same-process only)
34	  z_buz_colophon_roll=()
35	  z_buz_module_roll=()
36	  z_buz_command_roll=()
37	  z_buz_channel_roll=()
38	  z_buz_describe_roll=()
39	
40	  # Group rolls (populated by buz_group, consumed by buz_emit_context)
41	  z_buz_group_index_roll=()
42	  z_buz_group_prefix_roll=()
43	  z_buz_group_description_roll=()
44	
45	  readonly ZBUZ_KINDLED=1
46	}
47	
48	######################################################################
49	# Internal sentinel
50	
51	zbuz_sentinel() {
52	  test "${ZBUZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buz not kindled - call zbuz_kindle first"
53	}
54	
55	######################################################################
56	# Internal helpers
57	
58	# zbuz_resolve_tabtarget_capture() - Resolve colophon to tabtarget path
59	# Args: colophon
60	# Returns: tabtarget path or exit 1
61	zbuz_resolve_tabtarget_capture() {
62	  zbuz_sentinel
63	
64	  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
65	  test -n "${z_colophon}" || return 1
66	
67	  local z_matches=("${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/${z_colophon}."*.sh)
68	
69	  # Bash 3.2: no-match glob returns literal — check with test -e
70	  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || return 1
71	
72	  # Allow multiple matches (imprinted colophons share a colophon prefix)
73	  # Return first match as representative
74	  echo "${z_matches[0]}"
75	}
76	
77	######################################################################
78	# Group declaration (category metadata for context generation)
79	
80	# buz_group() - Declare a colophon group category
81	# Args: constant_name, prefix, description
82	# Stores group metadata at the current roll position for buz_emit_context.
83	buz_group() {
84	  zbuz_sentinel
85	
86	  local -r z_constant="${1:-}"
87	  local -r z_prefix="${2:-}"
88	  local -r z_description="${3:-}"
89	  test -n "${z_constant}"    || buc_die "buz_group: constant name required"
90	  test -n "${z_prefix}"      || buc_die "buz_group: prefix required"
91	  test -n "${z_description}" || buc_die "buz_group: description required"
92	
93	  z_buz_group_index_roll+=("${#z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}")
94	  z_buz_group_prefix_roll+=("${z_prefix}")
95	  z_buz_group_description_roll+=("${z_description}")
96	}
97	
98	######################################################################
99	# Public enroll (kindle-only registry population)
100	
101	# buz_enroll() - Register colophon tuple in parallel rolls
102	# Args: varname, colophon, module, command, channel, description
103	# All 6 arguments required. Channel: "" (none), "imprint", or "param1".
104	# Assigns colophon string to caller's variable via printf -v
105	# Side effects: populates registry rolls (must be called in same process, NOT inside $())
106	buz_enroll() {
107	  zbuz_sentinel
108	
109	  test $# -eq 6 || buc_die "buz_enroll: requires 6 arguments (varname colophon module command channel description), got $#"
110	
111	  local -r z_varname="${1}"
112	  local -r z_colophon="${2}"
113	  local -r z_module="${3}"
114	  local -r z_command="${4}"
115	  local -r z_channel="${5}"
116	  local -r z_description="${6}"
117	  test -n "${z_varname}"     || buc_die "buz_enroll: varname required"
118	  test -n "${z_colophon}"    || buc_die "buz_enroll: colophon required"
119	  test -n "${z_module}"      || buc_die "buz_enroll: module required"
120	  test -n "${z_command}"     || buc_die "buz_enroll: command required"
121	  test -n "${z_description}" || buc_die "buz_enroll: description required"
122	
123	  # Validate channel value (empty string is valid — means no channel)
124	  case "${z_channel}" in
125	    ""|"imprint"|"param1") ;;
126	    *) buc_die "buz_enroll: invalid channel: ${z_channel}" ;;
127	  esac
128	
129	  # Validate variable name
130	  [[ "${z_varname}" =~ ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ ]] \
131	    || buc_die "buz_enroll: invalid variable name: ${z_varname}"
132	
133	  # Roll population (only persists in same-process context, lost in $() subshell)
134	  z_buz_colophon_roll+=("${z_colophon}")
135	  z_buz_module_roll+=("${z_module}")
136	  z_buz_command_roll+=("${z_command}")
137	  z_buz_channel_roll+=("${z_channel}")
138	  z_buz_describe_roll+=("${z_description}")
139	
140	  # Assign colophon to caller's variable
141	  printf -v "${z_varname}" '%s' "${z_colophon}" || buc_die "buz_enroll: printf -v failed for ${z_varname}"
142	}
143	
144	######################################################################
145	# Context generation (emit markdown from registry metadata)
146	
147	# buz_emit_context() - Emit colophon registry as markdown, organized by groups
148	# Args: tabtarget_dir
149	# Writes markdown to stdout. Groups come from buz_group; descriptions from buz_enroll.
150	# Frontispiece extracted from tabtarget filenames on disk.
151	buz_emit_context() {
152	  zbuz_sentinel
153	
154	  local -r z_tt_dir="${1:-}"
155	  test -n "${z_tt_dir}" || buc_die "buz_emit_context: tabtarget directory required"
156	
157	  printf '%s\n' "## Command Reference (Generated)"
158	  printf '%s\n' ""
159	  printf '%s\n' "<!-- Generated by buz_emit_context from zipper registry. Do not edit. -->"
160	  printf '%s\n' "<!-- Regenerate: tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh -->"
161	  printf '%s\n' ""
162	  printf '%s\n' "**Folio** is the runtime target value passed to a command (nameplate moniker, role name, etc.)."
163	  printf '%s\n' "The Folio column shows how each tabtarget receives it:"
164	  printf '%s\n' ""
165	  printf '%s\n' "- **imprint**: Folio is baked into the filename — one tabtarget per target (e.g., \`tt/rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh\`)"
166	  printf '%s\n' "- **param1**: Folio is passed as a command-line argument (e.g., \`tt/rbw-cKB.KludgeBottle.sh tadmor\`)"
167	  printf '%s\n' "- **—**: No folio needed — standalone command"
168	  printf '%s\n' ""
169	
170	  local z_group_cursor=0
171	  local z_in_table=0
172	  local z_i
173	
174	  for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
175	    # Emit group header when we reach its starting index
176	    if (( z_group_cursor < ${#z_buz_group_index_roll[@]} )) \
177	       && (( z_i == z_buz_group_index_roll[z_group_cursor] )); then
178	      if (( z_in_table )); then
179	        printf '%s\n' ""
180	      fi
181	      printf '### %s (`%s`)\n' \
182	        "${z_buz_group_description_roll[z_group_cursor]}" \
183	        "${z_buz_group_prefix_roll[z_group_cursor]}"
184	      printf '%s\n' ""
185	      printf '%s\n' "| Colophon | Frontispiece | Folio | Purpose |"
186	      printf '%s\n' "|----------|-------------|-------|---------|"
187	      z_in_table=1
188	      z_group_cursor=$((z_group_cursor + 1))
189	    fi
190	
191	    # Resolve frontispiece from tabtarget filename
192	    local z_colophon="${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}"
193	    local z_frontispiece=""
194	    local z_matches=("${z_tt_dir}/${z_colophon}."*.sh)
195	    if test -e "${z_matches[0]}"; then
196	      local z_basename="${z_matches[0]##*/}"
197	      local z_stem="${z_basename%.sh}"
198	      local z_skip=$(( ${#z_colophon} + 1 ))
199	      local z_after="${z_stem:z_skip}"
200	      z_frontispiece="${z_after%%.*}"
201	    fi
202	
203	    local z_channel="${z_buz_channel_roll[z_i]:-}"
204	    local z_folio_display="${z_channel:-—}"
205	    local z_desc="${z_buz_describe_roll[z_i]:-}"
206	    printf '| `%s` | %s | %s | %s |\n' "${z_colophon}" "${z_frontispiece}" "${z_folio_display}" "${z_desc}"
207	  done
208	
209	  if (( z_in_table )); then
210	    printf '%s\n' ""
211	  fi
212	}
213	
214	######################################################################
215	# Healthcheck (opt-in tabtarget validation — called by consumer, not by BUK)
216	
217	# buz_healthcheck() - Validate that all enrolled colophons have tabtargets on disk
218	# Dies on first missing tabtarget. Call after enrollment is complete.
219	buz_healthcheck() {
220	  zbuz_sentinel
221	
222	  local z_i=""
223	  for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
224	    zbuz_resolve_tabtarget_capture "${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}" >/dev/null \
225	      || buc_die "buz_healthcheck: no tabtarget for colophon '${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}' in ${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/"
226	  done
227	}
228	
229	######################################################################
230	# Lookup dispatch
231	
232	# buz_exec_lookup() - Resolve colophon via registry and exec
233	# Args: colophon, base_dir [, extra args passed through to exec]
234	# Execs: BUZ_FOLIO=<folio> ${base_dir}/${module} ${command} [extra args]
235	# Dies if colophon not found
236	buz_exec_lookup() {
237	  zbuz_sentinel
238	
239	  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
240	  local z_base_dir="${2:-}"
241	  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: colophon required"
242	  test -n "${z_base_dir}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: base_dir required"
243	  shift 2
244	
245	  # Find colophon in registry
246	  local z_found=""
247	  local z_i=""
248	  for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
249	    test "${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}" = "${z_colophon}" || continue
250	    z_found="${z_i}"
251	    break
252	  done
253	  test -n "${z_found}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: colophon not found: ${z_colophon}"
254	
255	  # Decode folio from channel
256	  local z_folio=""
257	  local z_args=("$@")
258	  case "${z_buz_channel_roll[z_found]}" in
259	    "") ;;
260	    "imprint")
261	      z_folio="${BURD_TOKEN_3}"
262	      ;;
263	    "param1")
264	      if (( ${#z_args[@]} )); then
265	        z_folio="${z_args[0]}"
266	        z_args=("${z_args[@]:1}")
267	      fi
268	      ;;
269	    *)
270	      buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: unknown channel: ${z_buz_channel_roll[z_found]}"
271	      ;;
272	  esac
273	
274	  # Dispatch with folio in exec environment
275	  BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec "${z_base_dir}/${z_buz_module_roll[z_found]}" "${z_buz_command_roll[z_found]}" ${z_args[@]+"${z_args[@]}"}
276	}
277	
278	# eof
279

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUK Zipper - Colophon registry via parallel arrays with regime-selection channels

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBUZ_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module buz multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBUZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal kindle boilerplate

zbuz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "buz already kindled"

  # Registry rolls (populated by buz_enroll in consumer kindle, same-process only)
  z_buz_colophon_roll=()
  z_buz_module_roll=()
  z_buz_command_roll=()
  z_buz_channel_roll=()
  z_buz_describe_roll=()

  # Group rolls (populated by buz_group, consumed by buz_emit_context)
  z_buz_group_index_roll=()
  z_buz_group_prefix_roll=()
  z_buz_group_description_roll=()

  readonly ZBUZ_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Internal sentinel

zbuz_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buz not kindled - call zbuz_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Internal helpers

# zbuz_resolve_tabtarget_capture() - Resolve colophon to tabtarget path
# Args: colophon
# Returns: tabtarget path or exit 1
zbuz_resolve_tabtarget_capture() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_colophon}" || return 1

  local z_matches=("${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/${z_colophon}."*.sh)

  # Bash 3.2: no-match glob returns literal — check with test -e
  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || return 1

  # Allow multiple matches (imprinted colophons share a colophon prefix)
  # Return first match as representative
  echo "${z_matches[0]}"
}

######################################################################
# Group declaration (category metadata for context generation)

# buz_group() - Declare a colophon group category
# Args: constant_name, prefix, description
# Stores group metadata at the current roll position for buz_emit_context.
buz_group() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  local -r z_constant="${1:-}"
  local -r z_prefix="${2:-}"
  local -r z_description="${3:-}"
  test -n "${z_constant}"    || buc_die "buz_group: constant name required"
  test -n "${z_prefix}"      || buc_die "buz_group: prefix required"
  test -n "${z_description}" || buc_die "buz_group: description required"

  z_buz_group_index_roll+=("${#z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}")
  z_buz_group_prefix_roll+=("${z_prefix}")
  z_buz_group_description_roll+=("${z_description}")
}

######################################################################
# Public enroll (kindle-only registry population)

# buz_enroll() - Register colophon tuple in parallel rolls
# Args: varname, colophon, module, command, channel, description
# All 6 arguments required. Channel: "" (none), "imprint", or "param1".
# Assigns colophon string to caller's variable via printf -v
# Side effects: populates registry rolls (must be called in same process, NOT inside $())
buz_enroll() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  test $# -eq 6 || buc_die "buz_enroll: requires 6 arguments (varname colophon module command channel description), got $#"

  local -r z_varname="${1}"
  local -r z_colophon="${2}"
  local -r z_module="${3}"
  local -r z_command="${4}"
  local -r z_channel="${5}"
  local -r z_description="${6}"
  test -n "${z_varname}"     || buc_die "buz_enroll: varname required"
  test -n "${z_colophon}"    || buc_die "buz_enroll: colophon required"
  test -n "${z_module}"      || buc_die "buz_enroll: module required"
  test -n "${z_command}"     || buc_die "buz_enroll: command required"
  test -n "${z_description}" || buc_die "buz_enroll: description required"

  # Validate channel value (empty string is valid — means no channel)
  case "${z_channel}" in
    ""|"imprint"|"param1") ;;
    *) buc_die "buz_enroll: invalid channel: ${z_channel}" ;;
  esac

  # Validate variable name
  [[ "${z_varname}" =~ ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ ]] \
    || buc_die "buz_enroll: invalid variable name: ${z_varname}"

  # Roll population (only persists in same-process context, lost in $() subshell)
  z_buz_colophon_roll+=("${z_colophon}")
  z_buz_module_roll+=("${z_module}")
  z_buz_command_roll+=("${z_command}")
  z_buz_channel_roll+=("${z_channel}")
  z_buz_describe_roll+=("${z_description}")

  # Assign colophon to caller's variable
  printf -v "${z_varname}" '%s' "${z_colophon}" || buc_die "buz_enroll: printf -v failed for ${z_varname}"
}

######################################################################
# Context generation (emit markdown from registry metadata)

# buz_emit_context() - Emit colophon registry as markdown, organized by groups
# Args: tabtarget_dir
# Writes markdown to stdout. Groups come from buz_group; descriptions from buz_enroll.
# Frontispiece extracted from tabtarget filenames on disk.
buz_emit_context() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  local -r z_tt_dir="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_tt_dir}" || buc_die "buz_emit_context: tabtarget directory required"

  printf '%s\n' "## Command Reference (Generated)"
  printf '%s\n' ""
  printf '%s\n' "<!-- Generated by buz_emit_context from zipper registry. Do not edit. -->"
  printf '%s\n' "<!-- Regenerate: tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh -->"
  printf '%s\n' ""
  printf '%s\n' "**Folio** is the runtime target value passed to a command (nameplate moniker, role name, etc.)."
  printf '%s\n' "The Folio column shows how each tabtarget receives it:"
  printf '%s\n' ""
  printf '%s\n' "- **imprint**: Folio is baked into the filename — one tabtarget per target (e.g., \`tt/rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh\`)"
  printf '%s\n' "- **param1**: Folio is passed as a command-line argument (e.g., \`tt/rbw-cKB.KludgeBottle.sh tadmor\`)"
  printf '%s\n' "- **—**: No folio needed — standalone command"
  printf '%s\n' ""

  local z_group_cursor=0
  local z_in_table=0
  local z_i

  for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
    # Emit group header when we reach its starting index
    if (( z_group_cursor < ${#z_buz_group_index_roll[@]} )) \
       && (( z_i == z_buz_group_index_roll[z_group_cursor] )); then
      if (( z_in_table )); then
        printf '%s\n' ""
      fi
      printf '### %s (`%s`)\n' \
        "${z_buz_group_description_roll[z_group_cursor]}" \
        "${z_buz_group_prefix_roll[z_group_cursor]}"
      printf '%s\n' ""
      printf '%s\n' "| Colophon | Frontispiece | Folio | Purpose |"
      printf '%s\n' "|----------|-------------|-------|---------|"
      z_in_table=1
      z_group_cursor=$((z_group_cursor + 1))
    fi

    # Resolve frontispiece from tabtarget filename
    local z_colophon="${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}"
    local z_frontispiece=""
    local z_matches=("${z_tt_dir}/${z_colophon}."*.sh)
    if test -e "${z_matches[0]}"; then
      local z_basename="${z_matches[0]##*/}"
      local z_stem="${z_basename%.sh}"
      local z_skip=$(( ${#z_colophon} + 1 ))
      local z_after="${z_stem:z_skip}"
      z_frontispiece="${z_after%%.*}"
    fi

    local z_channel="${z_buz_channel_roll[z_i]:-}"
    local z_folio_display="${z_channel:-—}"
    local z_desc="${z_buz_describe_roll[z_i]:-}"
    printf '| `%s` | %s | %s | %s |\n' "${z_colophon}" "${z_frontispiece}" "${z_folio_display}" "${z_desc}"
  done

  if (( z_in_table )); then
    printf '%s\n' ""
  fi
}

######################################################################
# Healthcheck (opt-in tabtarget validation — called by consumer, not by BUK)

# buz_healthcheck() - Validate that all enrolled colophons have tabtargets on disk
# Dies on first missing tabtarget. Call after enrollment is complete.
buz_healthcheck() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
    zbuz_resolve_tabtarget_capture "${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}" >/dev/null \
      || buc_die "buz_healthcheck: no tabtarget for colophon '${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}' in ${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}/"
  done
}

######################################################################
# Lookup dispatch

# buz_exec_lookup() - Resolve colophon via registry and exec
# Args: colophon, base_dir [, extra args passed through to exec]
# Execs: BUZ_FOLIO=<folio> ${base_dir}/${module} ${command} [extra args]
# Dies if colophon not found
buz_exec_lookup() {
  zbuz_sentinel

  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
  local z_base_dir="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: colophon required"
  test -n "${z_base_dir}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: base_dir required"
  shift 2

  # Find colophon in registry
  local z_found=""
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_buz_colophon_roll[@]}"; do
    test "${z_buz_colophon_roll[z_i]}" = "${z_colophon}" || continue
    z_found="${z_i}"
    break
  done
  test -n "${z_found}" || buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: colophon not found: ${z_colophon}"

  # Decode folio from channel
  local z_folio=""
  local z_args=("$@")
  case "${z_buz_channel_roll[z_found]}" in
    "") ;;
    "imprint")
      z_folio="${BURD_TOKEN_3}"
      ;;
    "param1")
      if (( ${#z_args[@]} )); then
        z_folio="${z_args[0]}"
        z_args=("${z_args[@]:1}")
      fi
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "buz_exec_lookup: unknown channel: ${z_buz_channel_roll[z_found]}"
      ;;
  esac

  # Dispatch with folio in exec environment
  BUZ_FOLIO="${z_folio}" exec "${z_base_dir}/${z_buz_module_roll[z_found]}" "${z_buz_command_roll[z_found]}" ${z_args[@]+"${z_args[@]}"}
}

# eof

770	[[burh_command]]
771	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
772	{burh_command}::
773	Shell command for `command=` routing in SSH `authorized_keys`.
774	Determines which shell or environment the connection launches
775	(e.g., `C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l` for Cygwin, `wsl.exe -d Ubuntu` for WSL).
776	
777	=== BURD Regime
778	
779	[[burd_prefix]]
780	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
781	{burd_prefix}::
782	Bash Utility Dispatch Runtime
783	
784	[[burd_regime]]
785	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
786	{burd_regime}::
787	Ephemeral runtime state assembled by bud_dispatch.sh during tabtarget execution.
788	Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURD variables are constructed inline — no assignment file exists.
789	The dispatch main function IS the kindle: zbud_setup sources configuration and derives computed state, zbud_process_args parses the tabtarget into tokens.
790	
791	==== Input Variables
792	
793	[[burd_group_input]]
794	//axl_voices axhrgb_group
795	{burd_group_input}::
796	Variables set by the caller before dispatch invocation.
797	
798	[[burd_regime_file]]
799	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
800	{burd_regime_file}::
801	Path to the BURC regime assignment file to source during setup.
802	
803	[[burd_no_log]]
804	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
805	{burd_no_log}::
806	When non-empty, suppresses all log file creation and output.
807	
808	[[burd_interactive]]
809	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
810	{burd_interactive}::
811	When non-empty, enables interactive mode with uncurated tee logging.
812	
813	[[burd_coordinator_script]]
814	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
815	{burd_coordinator_script}::
816	Path to the coordinator script (workbench/testbench) that handles the dispatched command.
817	
818	[[burd_launcher]]
819	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
820	{burd_launcher}::
821	Path to the launcher bootstrap script, set by the tabtarget before exec.
822	
823	[[burd_station_file]]
824	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
825	{burd_station_file}::
826	Absolute path to the developer's station regime assignment file.
827	Set by the launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE.
828	
829	[[burd_term_cols]]
830	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
831	{burd_term_cols}::
832	Terminal column width at dispatch time.
833	Detected from stty if available, defaults to 80.
834	
835	==== Computed Variables
836	
837	[[burd_group_computed]]
838	//axl_voices axhrgb_group
839	{burd_group_computed}::
840	Variables derived during dispatch setup.
841	
842	[[burd_now_stamp]]
843	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
844	{burd_now_stamp}::
845	Unique timestamp combining date, time, PID, and random component.
846	
847	[[burd_now_epoch]]
848	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
849	{burd_now_epoch}::

file.content:
[[burh_command]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burh_command}::
Shell command for `command=` routing in SSH `authorized_keys`.
Determines which shell or environment the connection launches
(e.g., `C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l` for Cygwin, `wsl.exe -d Ubuntu` for WSL).

=== BURD Regime

[[burd_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burd_prefix}::
Bash Utility Dispatch Runtime

[[burd_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{burd_regime}::
Ephemeral runtime state assembled by bud_dispatch.sh during tabtarget execution.
Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURD variables are constructed inline — no assignment file exists.
The dispatch main function IS the kindle: zbud_setup sources configuration and derives computed state, zbud_process_args parses the tabtarget into tokens.

==== Input Variables

[[burd_group_input]]
//axl_voices axhrgb_group
{burd_group_input}::
Variables set by the caller before dispatch invocation.

[[burd_regime_file]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_regime_file}::
Path to the BURC regime assignment file to source during setup.

[[burd_no_log]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burd_no_log}::
When non-empty, suppresses all log file creation and output.

[[burd_interactive]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burd_interactive}::
When non-empty, enables interactive mode with uncurated tee logging.

[[burd_coordinator_script]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_coordinator_script}::
Path to the coordinator script (workbench/testbench) that handles the dispatched command.

[[burd_launcher]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_launcher}::
Path to the launcher bootstrap script, set by the tabtarget before exec.

[[burd_station_file]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_station_file}::
Absolute path to the developer's station regime assignment file.
Set by the launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE.

[[burd_term_cols]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{burd_term_cols}::
Terminal column width at dispatch time.
Detected from stty if available, defaults to 80.

==== Computed Variables

[[burd_group_computed]]
//axl_voices axhrgb_group
{burd_group_computed}::
Variables derived during dispatch setup.

[[burd_now_stamp]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_now_stamp}::
Unique timestamp combining date, time, PID, and random component.

[[burd_now_epoch]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{burd_now_epoch}::
UTC epoch seconds captured from same `date` invocation as {burd_now_stamp}.

[[burd_temp_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_temp_dir}::
Ephemeral directory for this dispatch invocation.

[[burd_output_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_output_dir}::
Fixed output directory cleared and recreated on each dispatch.

[[burd_transcript]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_transcript}::
Path to the transcript file within the temporary directory.

[[burd_git_context]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_git_context}::

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURH Regime - Bash Utility Regime Host Module
#
# BURH is a multi-instance regime — one file per SSH connection profile.
# Each profile lives at .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burh.env

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURH_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burh multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURH_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburh_*)

zburh_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURH_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burh already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all BURH variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURH

  buv_group_enroll "Connection Identity"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_HOST        1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote machine"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURH_USER        1   32  "Username on the remote host"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURH_ALIAS       1   64  "SSH alias and key filename (must match directory name)"

  buv_group_enroll "Authentication"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_SSH_PUBKEY  20 1024  "Full public key line (e.g., ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host)"

  buv_group_enroll "Session Routing"
  buv_string_enroll  BURH_COMMAND      1  512  "Shell command for command= routing in authorized_keys"

  # Guard against unexpected BURH_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURH BURH_

  # Lock all enrolled BURH_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURH

  readonly ZBURH_KINDLED=1
}

zburh_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURH_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burh not kindled - call zburh_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURH enrollment validations
zburh_enforce() {
  zburh_sentinel

  buv_vet BURH
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (burh_*)

# List available alias names as space-separated tokens
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (needs BURS_USER), BURD kindled (needs BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burh_list_capture() {
  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}"
  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1

  local z_result=""
  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burh.env)
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_dir##*/}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}
